nodes.itemHTML: • ziglibs/s2sstars61 - Binary serialization format and library.
• Beatbump - An alternative front-end for Youtube Music without Ads.
• axgdev/telegram_echobot_zigstars4 - Telegram echo bot.
• batiati/mustache-zigstars53 - Mustache implementation.
• reitzig/texlive-dockerstars5 - minimal TeXLive system with package installation possibility
• Island of TeX/texlive - debian-based full TeXLive system
• dante-ev/docker-texlivestars60 - debian-based full TeXLive system with addtions with pandoc, perl, and python pre-installed
• xu-cheng/latex-actionstars741 - GitHub Action to compile LaTeX documents
• dante-ev/latex-actionstars143 - DANTE e.V.'s GitHub action to compile LaTeX documents. Offers a full TeXLive and pre-installed perl and python.
• One Key Linux Setupstars7 - Simple setup (ubuntu-only) of zsh, oh-my-zsh, tmux, python support and other packages.
• Robert Robere. Proof Complexity: Algorithms and Lower Bounds - An introduction to modern proof complexity, emphasizing its connections with computational complexity and algorithms in optimization.
• Building Bridges I, Building Bridges II, Fete of Combinatorics and Computer Science - Collected works in celebration of Laszlo Lovasz, Connecting discrete math with computer science.

• Entropy, Search, Complexity - A collection of survey papers in the fields of entropy, search and complexity. It includes also search theory and quantum source coding. Recommended to both researchers and students.

• RemSupp — On-demand support and permanent access to devices (2 sessions/day for free)
• Mealiestars2.5k - Mealie is a self hosted recipe manager and meal planner.
• ada-runtimestars31 - A downsized Ada runtime which can be adapted to different platforms.
• pasta - Pasta! is a game of the "match-3" genre, implemented in gnoga.
• buttonsstars2 - A simple gtkadastars73 Button Mania game.
• play-2048stars2 - Play 2048! is a clone of the popular 2048 game, implemented in Ada using asfmlstars19 for graphics and ada-tomlstars24 for saving state.
• bingadastars4 - Bingo application in gtkadastars73

• gneissstars22 - Gneiss is an interface collection to be used with applications for component based systems. It aims to be easily portable/platform independent and is compatible with the ada-runtimestars31
• gnoga - The GNU Omnificent GUI for Ada.
• gtkadastars73 - Ada graphical toolkit based on Gtk3 components.
• matreshka - Matreshka is an Ada framework to develop information systems. It consists of five major components: League, XML processor, Web framework, SQL access, and the Modeling framework.
• zip-ada - Zip-Ada is a programming library for dealing with the Zip compressed archive file format.
• dcf-ada - An Ada 2012 library for document container files (Based on zip-ada).
• protobuf-adastars9 - Experimental Ada code generation support for Google Protocol Buffers.
• protobufstars16 - A Google Protocol Buffers implementation in Ada, using matreshka.
• ada-tomlstars24 - TOML parser for Ada.
• template-parserstars4 - AWS templates engine.
• asfmlstars19 - Ada binding to the Simple and Fast Multimedia Library.
• ssprepstars0 - An extensible template engine akin to jinja but using template-parserstars4 and intended for command line usage.
• hunterstars21 - Graphical (gtkadastars73) file manager for Linux.
• eagle-landerstars26 - Apollo 11 lunar lander simulator (gtkadastars73/Cairo).
• a2js - Ada2JS is experimental open source compiler, component of matreshka.
• tp7-ada-gtkadastars3 - Implementation of Turbo Pascal 7.0 units with gtkadastars73.
• tp7-ada-gnogastars4 - Implementation of Turbo Pascal 7.0 units with gnoga.
• MikTeX - Most common LaTeX distribution for Windows, but also available for Mac, Linux or as Docker image.
• XeTeX - TeX compiler that provides better unicode and font support than TeX/pdfTeX (i.e. you can use the fonts of your operating system instead of only TeX fonts).

• Texpad - Commercial LaTeX editor for macOS and iOS, with excellent features (document overview, synchronised PDF display, autocompletion, sync across devices, etc.) that never get in the way of writing.
• Bibdesk - Great bibliography editor for .
• Codecogs Eqn Editor - Online LaTeX equation editor that allows you to produce figures containing an equation.
• EqualX - Graphical LaTeX formula editor.
• LaTeXEqEdit - LaTeX formula editor for Windows.
• csv2latex - Converts CSV files from your favorite programs to LaTeX tabulars.
• pgfplots - Truly awesome plotting library on top of and in the style of TikZ/pgf. This library can load in CSV data files, perform some calculations and create beautiful plots.
• BibTeX Style Examples - Example output of common BibTeX styles (BST files).
• Begin LaTeX in minutesstars3.2k - Brief intro to LaTeX for beginners that helps you use LaTeX with ease.
• GeoGebra - Cross-platform geometry tool with output to TikZ.
• LearnLaTeX.org - Browser-based LaTeX tutorial.
• LuaTeX - (La)TeX compiler that supports Lua code for scripting and has improved unicode and font support than standard TeX/pdfTeX.
• TeXMaker - Pretty good alternative to Kile.
• WinEdt - The LaTeX editor many people swear by. Only for .
• TeXnicCenter - Quite old but free and decent editor for LaTeX.
• TeXShop - No-nonsense editor for LaTeX documents which is included in MacTeX.
• BakomaTex - Commercial LaTeX editor that allows to edit your document both using its source code and WYSIWYG.
• Inlage - Commercial LaTeX editor with handwritten formula recognition, Excel importing and more nifty features.

• Vim
    • Vim-LaTeX
    • LaTeX Live Previewstars764 - Instantly previews your LaTeX document.
    • vimtexstars4.2k - Modern vim plugin for editing LaTeX files. Has a variety of features including live preview and forward search.
• TikzEdt (also: GitHub repostars8) - WYSIWYG and text-based editor for TikZ pictures.
• IPE - Drawing tool that integrates well with LaTeX commands and documents.
• TeXample - Blog about LaTeX, with a big collection of TikZ figures.
• tkz - Collection of TikZ-based packages to make plots and graphs.
• minted - The minted package uses pygments to generate the listings. In this way, LaTeX is able to format more than 300 programming and markup languages and other text formats.
• Comprehensive LaTeX symbol list - Very extensive list of symbols for LaTeX. Available in A4 and letter sizes.
• Detexify - You draw the symbol and this site/app will tell you the LaTeX command.
• TeXDoc - Online interface to the texdoc utility to browse LaTeX packages and documentation.
• Dickimaw Books: LaTeX resources - Great overview of resources useful for LaTeX.
• LaTeX cookbook - Sibling of TeXample, contains quite a bit of example code.
• LaTeX community - Forum about LaTeX.
• TeX World - Websites supported by the TeX Users Group, DANTE, and GUTenberg.
• The (Not So) Short Introduction to LaTeX2e - Very comprehensive introduction to LaTeX.

• Formatting Information (2020) - This is the HTML5-based online version of the book Formatting Information - An introduction to typesetting with LATEX. It has been continuosuly updated since the early 2000s.
• TeXblog - Blog about LaTeX and everything related.
• TeX Talk - Blog for the TeX Stack Exchange site with news and interviews.
• Arara (GitHub repostars344) - Simple tool that allows you to specify which tools to call inside your document and it can be extended quite easily.
• Zotero - Reference manager for your browser that also exports to bibtex and integrates with many LaTeX editors.
• Papisstars897 - Extremely customizable, powerful and simple cross-platform (Python) library manager. It has a very complete Command-Line-Interface, several GUIs and scripting capability.
• Visual FAQ - Typesetting issues and a link to appropriate TeX FAQ answers.
• ChkTeX - Linter / code checker for LaTeX documents.

• Princeton's Companion. Advice to a Young Mathematician - Five contributors draw on their experiences of mathematical life and research, and to offer advice that they might have liked to receive when they were just setting-out on their careers.

• O'Donnell. CS Theory Toolkit - It covers a large number of the math/CS topics that you need to know for reading and doing research in Computer Science Theory - alternatively: bilibili
• Zhou. A Theorist's Toolkit - It covers a large number of the math/CS topics that you need to know for reading and doing research in Computer Science Theory.

• Arora. Thinking Like a Theorist - It covers a large number of the math/CS topics that you need to know for reading and doing research in Computer Science Theory.

• Arora. A Theorist's Toolkit - Aimed primarily at first and second year graduate students who plan to do research in theoretical computer science. We will introduce probabilistic, algebraic, combinatorial, and algorithmic methods useful in proofs.

• Arora. Advanced Algorithm Design - Notably uses ideas such as randomness, approximation, high dimensional geometry. Faces uncertainty, approaches to handle big data, handling intractability, heuristic approaches, ..etc.

• Arora. The Computational Universe - Takes us on a broad sweep of scientific knowledge and related technologies: propositional logic of the ancient Greeks (microprocessors); quantum mechanics (silicon chips); network and system phenomena (internet and search engines); computational intractability (secure encryption); and efficient algorithms (genomic sequencing).

• jachLogstars28. [Delphi] pure pascal, flexible, extensible and lightweight library to add logging capabilities to your Delphi applications. Multiple log destinations supported. The library supports multithreaded applications and is itself multithreaded to minimize the impact writing the log may have on the performance on mission critical applications.
• Jukna. Extremal Combinatorics - Combinatorial techniques written largely with an eye to their applications in TCS, and mostly in complexity

HPR is driven entirely by the content provided by it's community - on any subject, at any length, in any quality, as long as it's of "Interest to hackers" (basically anyone with any interest in technology). It is constantly actively looking for content and submissions.
